data.title: Navan Shares Gain as 4Q Revenue Tops Wall Street's Estimates
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Shares of Navan climbed after the company logged better-than-expected revenue and guided for growth continuing growth this fiscal year.

The stock surged 20% to $10.94 in after-hours trading on Wednesday. Through the market close shares have slid 42% in the past three months.

The business travel and expense platform company posted a fourth-quarter loss of $72.8 million, or 29 cents a share, compared with a loss of $46.7 million, or $1.03 a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings per share were 2 cents, compared with estimates for an adjusted loss of 22 cents a share, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

Revenue climbed 35% to $177.9 million, compared with analyst estimates of $162.2 million.

The increase was driven by growth in both usage and subscription revenue.

Chief Executive Ariel Cohen said the period was a landmark quarter as enterprise customers chose Navan's platform to replace legacy systems.

"With record-breaking go-to-market momentum, we believe we are better positioned than ever to redefine the travel and expense industry," Cohen said.

For the fiscal year, Navan expects revenue of $866 million to $874 million, or up 24% at the midpoint, compared with analysts' forecast of $840.8 million, according to FactSet.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: P3 Health Partners Enters Multi-Year Services Pact With Nebraska Nonprofit Insurer; Global Risk From 2028
data.description:
P3 Health Partners entered into a Master Services Agreement and Statement of Work with a large nonprofit health insurance provider in Nebraska, effective March 19, 2026. P3 will deliver clinical, operational and data-driven support to primary care providers in the insurer’s Medicare Advantage network, with management services fees for 2026–2027 and a shift to a global risk arrangement starting in 2028. The MSA runs through December 31, 2030 with automatic annual renewals and includes customary termination rights, a 90-day termination option tied to 2026 performance metrics or key-person departures, and a potential break-up fee if the client does not pursue a 2027–2028 Medicare Advantage bid.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Master Services Agreement and Statement of Work for Care Enablement services
  • Counterparty: Large nonprofit health insurance provider in Nebraska
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 19 2026 / Mar 19 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through Dec 31 2030, auto-renews annually
  • Reason: Expand Medicare Advantage enablement services with Nebraska partner

Original SEC Filing: P3 Health Partners Inc. [ PIII ] - 8-K - Mar. 25, 2026

data.title: Enerpac Tool Group reports Q2 fiscal 2026: Revenue $154.8M, Adj. diluted EPS $0.39
data.description:
Enerpac Tool Group reported second-quarter fiscal 2026 results with net sales of $154.8 million and net earnings of $16.3 million (diluted EPS $0.31). Adjusted diluted EPS was $0.39 and adjusted EBITDA totaled $33.0 million for the quarter. Management noted product strength in Industrial Tool & Service and actions to restructure EMEA service operations amid softer demand.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: Net sales of $154.8 million for the three months ended February 28, 2026 (up 6% year-over-year; organic sales +2%).
  • Gross profit and margin: Gross profit $71.8 million; gross profit margin 46.4% (decline of 410 basis points year-over-year).
  • Operating income: Operating profit of $25.0 million for the quarter.
  • Net income and EPS: Net earnings $16.3 million; diluted EPS $0.31 for the quarter.
  • Adjusted results: Adjusted diluted EPS $0.39 and adjusted EBITDA $33.0 million (adjusted EBITDA margin 21.3%).

Business Highlights

  • Product vs. service mix: IT&S product sales increased 6% organically — the strongest quarterly product growth in 10 quarters — while IT&S service revenue declined 17% organically, driven by softness in EMEA.
  • Regional performance: Management reported mid-single-digit growth in order rates with gains across all three regions, offset by EMEA service weakness prompting a regional restructuring.
  • Contracts and commercial wins: Secured a five-year service contract with a major UK oil & gas customer, expected to provide several million dollars in annual revenue.
  • New product launches and M&A integration: Launched six new products at ConExpo, including the acquired Hydra Pac diesel split flow pump; M&A-related charges recorded in the quarter reflect ongoing integration activities.
  • Capital allocation and shareholder returns: Repurchased approximately 1.3 million shares in the quarter for $51 million under the existing repurchase program.

Original SEC Filing: ENERPAC TOOL GROUP CORP [ EPAC ] - 8-K - Mar. 25, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Lionheart Holdings 10-K: Net income $8.95M (2025) and $5.84M (2024) from trust interest
data.description:
Lionheart Holdings reported net income of $8.95 million for 2025 and $5.84 million for 2024, driven primarily by interest income earned on its IPO trust account offset by modest operating costs; traditional revenue, gross profit and operating income line items are not presented. The company remains a Cayman Islands blank-check sponsor focused on completing an initial business combination by June 20, 2026, and used its trust proceeds to support target search and public-company readiness. Management notes that interest on the trust was the principal source of reported earnings while operating activity to date has been pre-combination in nature.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income (2025): $8.95 million, consisting primarily of $9.83 million interest income on the Trust Account minus $0.87 million operating costs.
  • Net Income (2024): $5.84 million, consisting primarily of $6.34 million interest income on the Trust Account minus $0.50 million operating costs.
  • Revenue / Sales / Gross Profit / Operating Income / Net Income Per Share: Not reported as separate line items in Part II Item 7/Item 8; filings disclose only interest income and operating costs.

Business Highlights

  • Business focus: Lionheart Holdings is a Cayman Islands blank-check special purpose acquisition company formed to complete an initial business combination within 24 months (by June 20, 2026).
  • Capital deployment: Approximately $230 million was placed in a trust from the IPO and related private placement to fund a target acquisition and provide post-combination working capital.
  • Operational activity: Pre-combination activity has centered on target search, due diligence and preparing for public-company readiness; the company has not generated operating revenues from an acquired business.
  • Management and support: Management is targeting established, scalable businesses with experienced operating teams; sponsor and affiliates provide administrative and support services.
  • Liquidity and outlook: The company generated interest income on its trust account; management plans to consummate a business combination within the prescribed timeline or seek shareholder approval for an extension.

Original SEC Filing: Lionheart Holdings [ CUB ] - 10-K - Mar. 25, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Immix Biopharma 2025 10-K: Net loss $29.44M, EPS $(0.89)
data.description:
Immix Biopharma reported results for fiscal 2025 showing no operating profit as the company recorded a loss from operations of $29.956 million and a net loss attributable to common stockholders of $29.439 million. The weighted-average shares outstanding were 32,965,706, producing a loss per common share — basic and diluted — of $(0.89). These results reflect elevated clinical development activity and related operating expenses during the period.

Financial Highlights

  • Operating expenses: $29.956 million (total operating expenses reported for 2025)
  • Loss from operations: $(29.956) million (reported loss from operations for 2025)
  • Net loss attributable to common stockholders: $(29.439) million (net loss for 2025)
  • Loss per common share — basic and diluted: $(0.89) (weighted-average shares 32,965,706 for 2025)

Business Highlights

  • Clinical progress: Advanced lead CAR-T candidate NXC-201 through U.S. Phase 1b/2 and ex-U.S. Phase 1b/2a trials, with continued focus on enrollment milestones and clinical development.
  • Funding and capitalization: Completed multiple equity raises and at-the-market (ATM) sales in 2025 to provide cash resources to support expanded operations and clinical programs.
  • Grant support: Awarded an $8 million CIRM milestone-based grant, with $6.2 million received as of March 2026 to offset clinical development costs.
  • Operational build-out: Established a long-term leased biomanufacturing space and completed facility build-out to support clinical manufacturing activities.
  • R&D activity: Increased research and development spending in 2025 driven by higher clinical and CRO costs, reflecting intensified trial activity and site onboarding.

Original SEC Filing: Immix Biopharma, Inc. [ IMMX ] - 10-K - Mar. 25, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Axiom Intelligence Acquisition 10-K: $0.00 Revenue, Net Income $3.65M (EPS N/A)
data.description:
Axiom Intelligence Acquisition reported no operating revenues for the period and recorded net income of $3.65 million, driven primarily by $4.23 million of interest income partially offset by $0.585 million of general and administrative expenses. Gross profit and operating income are not applicable as the company had no operating activities or sales during the period. The results reflect early-stage SPAC activity centered on transaction search and corporate organization rather than commercial operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0.00 million (no operating revenues generated during the period)
  • Gross Profit: $0.00 million (no operating activities or sales)
  • Net Income: $3.65 million (comprised of $4.23 million interest income less $0.585 million G&A expenses)
  • Net Income Per Share: Not provided

Business Highlights

  • Business model: A focused special purpose acquisition company formed to complete a business combination targeting European infrastructure opportunities; currently evaluating targets and preparing for a Business Combination.
  • Capitalization & trust funding: $200 million placed in trust from IPO and private placement to fund the Business Combination and post-combination operations.
  • Operational activity: Early-stage operations limited to organization, target identification, due diligence, and IPO-related activities; no operating revenues yet.
  • Management outlook: Management anticipates increased public-company expenses after combination and may seek shareholder approval to extend the combination deadline; sponsor and public shareholder redemption rights remain preserved.
  • Milestones achieved: IPO closed June 20, 2025 (20 million units) with partial overallotment exercised; sponsors and certain investors purchased 600,000 private units.

data.title: Atossa Therapeutics 2025 10-K: $0.0M Revenue; Net loss $34.8M
data.description:
Atossa Therapeutics reported zero revenue for the year ended December 31, 2025 and recorded a net loss of $34.8 million, reflecting continued pre-revenue development-stage operations and increased clinical spending. The company reported operating expenses that produced an operating loss of $37.1 million and did not disclose a per-share net loss amount in the provided sections. These results underscore ongoing investment in clinical programs and reliance on external financing to fund near-term activities.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0.0 million, company reported no source of revenue for 2025
  • Gross Profit: $0.0 million, no cost of revenue reported for 2025
  • Operating Income: $(37.1) million, total operating expenses were $37.1 million for 2025
  • Net Income: $(34.8) million, recorded a net loss of $34.8 million for the year ended Dec 31, 2025
  • Net Income Per Share: Not reported as a per-share amount in provided sections; net loss disclosed only

Business Highlights

  • Clinical Progress: Advanced (Z)-endoxifen through multiple Phase 2 trials (Karisma, EVANGELINE, RECAST, I-SPY2) with reported promising efficacy signals.
  • Regulatory Momentum: Received FDA Rare Pediatric Disease and Orphan Drug designations for DMD and is pursuing additional orphan designations in 2026.
  • Operational Scale-up: Established a proprietary manufacturing process, qualified suppliers, and expanded leased office space to support development activities.
  • R&D Investment: Increased clinical and preclinical spending driving higher R&D activity and enrollment across neoadjuvant and breast density studies.
  • Capital & Go-Forward Plan: With no product revenue, the company is relying on equity offerings/ATM facility, grants, and collaborations to fund near-term clinical operations.

Original SEC Filing: ATOSSA THERAPEUTICS, INC. [ ATOS ] - 10-K - Mar. 25, 2026

data.title: Arverne Group: An Exceptional Year in 2025, Marked by Major Milestones
data.description:
+46% growth in gross business volume, reaching EUR25.2 million

Major milestones achieved

Drilling & Construction

  • Successful execution of operations for Groupe ADP and Safran
  • Completion of the first self-operated drilling operations on the Lithium de France project

Heating & Cooling:

  • First revenues generated from the Clichy-sous-Bois / Livry-Gargan project
  • First pre-contract signed, covering a gross business volume of EUR150 million over 30 years
  • Development of a 100-project commercial pipeline, representing 5 TWh of geothermal heat

Critical metals — Lithium de France project:

  • Successful launch of the pre-industrial phase, in particular marked by the drilling of the first geothermal well
  • Granting of a Green Industry Tax Credit (C3IV) for an amount of up to EUR150 million
  • EUR40 million Series B2 financing led by Arverne Group and its partner Equinor Ventures
  • Increase of Arverne Group's equity stake in Lithium de France to 73.8%

Strengthened commitments as a Mission-driven company

  • Avoided emissions, in line with the 2026 trajectory, tripled, with a 6% reduction in carbon intensity compared with 2024
  • 89% of employees satisfied, according to the Group's first employee engagement survey
  • Stronger local engagement through numerous local initiatives

Entry of a leading institutional shareholder

Bpifrance joins Arverne Group's shareholder base with a 4.06% stake, underscoring its confidence in the Group's growth strategy.

PAU, France--(BUSINESS WIRE)--March 25, 2026--

Regulatory News:

Arverne (FR001400JWR8 — ARVEN), France's leading provider of geothermal solutions, announces its 2025 annual results.

Pierre Brossollet, Founder and Chief Executive Officer of Arverne, commented:

"Driven by a collective commitment to French and European energy sovereignty, through the development of geothermal heat and geothermal lithium, Arverne is creating sustainable value in support of local communities and the energy transition.

In 2025, the Group's business volume increased by 46%, reaching EUR25.2 million, reflecting execution fully in line with the announced trajectory.

The commercial performance of the Heating & Cooling business was particularly strong and reflects accelerating momentum in 2026. The Group's portfolio, comprising nearly 100 deep geothermal projects, represents 5 TWh of potential, in line with the accelerated targets of France's Multiannual Energy Programme looking ahead to 2035.

In Critical metals, the Lithium de France project in Alsace is successfully continuing its pre-industrial phase, in particular through ongoing drilling operations. This structuring programme targets an annual production capacity of 27,000 tonnes of lithium, equivalent to equipping 800,000 electric vehicles, while also enhancing the attractiveness of local territories through the production of competitive, local and sustainable geothermal heat.

I would like to thank all Arverne teams, as well as our shareholders, for their exemplary commitment and mobilisation."

BUSINESS MOMENTUM AND PERFORMANCE BY SEGMENT

Gross business volume of EUR25.2 million in 2025, up sharply by +46% compared with 2024

 
In thousands of euros       2025    2024   Var. % 
Deep drilling             16,925  13,954  +21.3 % 
Heating & Cooling            844       -        - 
Others                       194     193    +1.0% 
------------------------  ------  ------  ------- 
Consolidated revenue      17,963  14,147   +27.0% 
------------------------  ------  ------  ------- 
Inter-segment drilling     4,737      54        - 
------------------------  ------  ------  ------- 
Surface drilling (50%)     2,541   3,092   -17.8% 
------------------------  ------  ------  ------- 
Gross business volume     25,241  17,293  +45.96% 
------------------------  ------  ------  -------

DRILLING & CONSTRUCTION

  • Deep Drilling -- Revenue

Consolidated revenue from the deep drilling business amounted to EUR16.93 million, up 21.3% compared with 2024, confirming the strength of the Group's operational execution.

Geothermal drilling operations carried out for Groupe ADP at Paris Charles de Gaulle Airport were completed in February 2025, in line with the original three-month schedule. These installations cover 35% of the site's heating requirements and avoid the emission of 19,000 tonnes of CO .

Drilling operations for Safran's future geothermal plant in Villaroche, one of its main industrial sites employing more than 5,000 people, began in November 2025 and will continue until the second quarter of 2026. By harnessing subsurface heat, this installation will cover 84% of the site's energy needs, while reducing carbon emissions by 75%, i.e. 6,500 tonnes of CO per year, and significantly lowering energy costs. These works represent the first operations carried out using the new B18 drilling rig, specifically designed for urban environments and low environmental impact.

Well maintenance operations conducted for Storengy, a leading industrial player in gas storage, continued in 2025, illustrating the recognition of the operational excellence of Arverne's teams. As from 2026, these operations will not be renewed, as the Group focuses its resources on the deployment of its strategic geothermal projects.

  • Inter-segment drilling -- Business volume

Self-operated drilling operations for the first geothermal well of the Lithium de France project began in late November 2025 and were successfully completed three months later. Drilling of the second well is scheduled for the second quarter of 2026.

These inter-segment drilling operations generated a gross business volume of EUR4.7 million in their first year, in 2025.

  • Surface drilling -- Business volume

DrillHeat's total revenue amounted to EUR5.1 million in 2025, down 18% compared with 2024, reflecting the postponement of projects to 2026 in a context of municipal elections.

Pursuing its value-chain integration strategy, DrillHeat has internally structured a specialised connection team, thereby strengthening its ability to deliver fully integrated and controlled solutions.

During the financial year, 21 surface drilling projects were carried out (vs. 27 in 2024), representing 40,000 metres drilled (vs. 48,000 metres in 2024) and an installed capacity of 2.0 MW (vs. 2.4 MW in 2024). Several flagship projects were delivered, including the E.Leclerc hypermarket in Frouard (Grand Est), the first 22,000 m(2) commercial site in France to integrate geothermal energy for all of its energy needs. DrillHeat also equipped the geothermal installations at the Sisley site in Vendôme, the Galeries Lafayette store in Pau — covering 90% of heating needs and 75% of cooling needs — as well as the Théâtre du Vellin in Villefontaine and a mail sorting platform.

HEATING & COOLING

  • A value-creating business model, scalable at large scale

Arverne has signed its first public service concession contract, in a consortium with Dalkia and SAS Île-de-France Énergies & Territoires, for the development and operation of a low-carbon district heating network in Clichy-sous-Bois and Livry-Gargan. This project integrates deep geothermal energy to supply urban heating networks and marks the first revenues of the Heat & Cooling business, for an amount of EUR0.8 million.

In addition, the Group has signed a pre-contract for a project representing EUR150 million in revenue over 30 years. This project covers the entire value chain, from deep drilling to the production and sales of geothermal heat, supplying 12,000 housing units. As the first SPV project led by Arverne and its partners, it represents EUR26 million in investments.

This SPV-based development model, led by Arverne Heat & Cooling and its partners, is designed to be replicated at scale.

  • Arverne strengthens its project portfolio, focused on deep geothermal energy in the Île-de-France region

Arverne's teams continued to strengthen the Group's commercial strategy, expanding the portfolio to nearly 100 projects across mainland France. As of end-2025, this portfolio represents a potential of approximately 5 TWh.

To optimise value creation, Arverne is primarily focusing its commercial efforts in the Île-de-France region, targeting deep geothermal projects for urban district heating networks, supported by long-term 30-year contracts.

As Europe's leading region for geothermal energy through district heating networks, with around 50 plants in operation, Île-de-France offers a major growth reservoir, supported by the resources of the Dogger basin and high urban density. Nearly half of the projects in the portfolio are currently located in the region.

  • Resources and strategic alliances

In line with the teams' expectations, contract signings slowed in the second half of 2025, due to increased caution among contracting authorities ahead of the municipal elections. A strong rebound in activity is expected as early as the second quarter of 2026.

In this context, Arverne Heating & Cooling significantly strengthened its internal resources in 2025 in preparation for the upcoming acceleration. To expand its territorial footprint, sales managers were recruited in Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Occitanie.

To optimise and accelerate commercial expansion, Arverne has deployed an effective partnership strategy in Nouvelle-Aquitaine:

  • Partnership with Crédit Agricole Pyrénées Gascogne Énergies Nouvelles (CAPGEN): CAPGEN will provide project financing, while Arverne will be responsible for the design, construction and operation of the facilities. The promotion will be carried out jointly by both partners.
  • Alliance with the Nouvelle-Aquitaine Region, through the Terra Énergies fund, alongside Banque des Territoires: Arverne will be responsible for assessing regional potential, conducting pre-feasibility studies, as well as project management and financing. By 2030, the Region aims to reach 45% renewable energy, notably through the deployment of district heating networks serving priority neighbourhoods and industrial sites. Terra Énergies and Arverne are already in advanced discussions with around ten local authorities, in coordination with regional services, to accelerate the development of geothermal energy, particularly in secondary schools.

CRITICAL METALS: LITHIUM DE FRANCE PROJECT

  • The pre-industrial phase is progressing successfully, in line with the planned timetable and conditions.

In 2025, the teams achieved the key milestones of the pre-industrial phase of the Lithium de France project in Alsace, combining geothermal heat and geothermal lithium.

  • Initial drilling operations: drilling the first well of the geothermal doublet, in Schwabwiller (Alsace), began in November 2025 and was completed in February 2026. Five years of preparatory work - from 3D seismic studies to environmental permitting procedures and securing the necessary authorisations - conducted by the teams of Lithium de France and Arverne Drilling & Works, enabled the project to achieve an unprecedented level of operational excellence for a drilling operation conducted under such conditions in Alsace.
  • DLE pilot plant: the pilot plant was assembled and installed on site in late 2025, in order to be operational as early as the first quarter of 2026 and to assess the performance of the innovative Direct Lithium Extraction (DLE) technology.
  • Progress on the Definitive Feasibility Study (DFS): Sedgman, one of the world's leading providers of mineral processing solutions and related infrastructure, was selected to carry out the Front-End Engineering Design (FEED), a key step in the Definitive Feasibility Study (DFS).
  • Bankability Feasibility Study (BFS): the study is being conducted under a co-mandate with Macquarie Capital and Crédit Agricole Corporate & Investment Bank, two leading institutions in financing the battery value chain.

The pre-industrial phase will continue until mid-2026.

  • Granting of a tax credit of up to EUR150 million to support green industry investments (C3IV)

Following a rigorous selection process, the Lithium de France project has been selected and will benefit from a tax credit aimed at encouraging reindustrialisation in France, while reducing greenhouse gas emissions and supporting a more decarbonised economy.

The Green Industry Investment Tax Credit (C3IV) is a French tax incentive introduced by the 2024 Finance Act to support sustainable industry. Eligible expenditures relate to a genuine industrialisation plan, through productive investments (plants, equipment, production processes, etc.).

Compared with conventional lithium currently available on the market, the geothermal lithium carbonate produced in Alsace by Arverne will enable a 70% reduction in carbon emissions, 2 to 3 times lower water consumption, and the creation of more than 200 direct jobs and approximately 700 indirect jobs.

  • Successful EUR40 million Series B2 financing

Arverne Group has completed the Series B2 financing of its subsidiary Lithium de France, together with its partner Equinor, the Norwegian energy major, through Equinor Ventures.

Equinor's geological expertise and experience in executing complex projects represent key assets for the project.

Arverne Group and Equinor Ventures are strengthening their partnership in a context of strong global lithium market growth - a critical metal for the energy transition, with demand set to rise by 10% per year through 2040(1) .

  • Arverne Group strengthens its stake in Lithium de France

Arverne Group carried out a share exchange with Hydro Energy to increase its ownership stake in Lithium de France.

The transaction was completed on 6 October 2025, through the issuance of 2,232,288 new Arverne Group shares to Hydro Energy Invest AS, for a total value of EUR22,322,880, corresponding to an Arverne Group share price of EUR10.00.

Following this transaction, Arverne Group holds 73.8% of the share capital of Lithium de France, Equinor Ventures holds 24.4%, and management holds 1.8%.

Hydro Energy Invest now holds 5.31% of Arverne Group's share capital.

2025 FINANCIAL RESULTS

  • Consolidated income statement: +27% revenue growth to EUR18.0 million

of which Group share                     (20, 712)   (9, 993)

of which minority share                   (2, 848)   (1, 133) 
---------------------------------------  ---------  ---------

The Group's consolidated revenue reached EUR17.96 million, representing a 27% year-on-year increase, underscoring the continued acceleration of business activity.

Capitalised production amounted to EUR10.55 million, up 62.2% compared with 2024. This increase is mainly attributable to the start of the drilling campaign for the Lithium de France project.

Consumed purchases, totalling EUR2.2 million, increased by 7.1%, reflecting higher drilling activity.

External expenses amounted to EUR21.2 million, up 26.7%, driven by the increase in third-party drilling activity, studies related to the Heat & Cooling business, and the start of drilling operations for Lithium de France.

Personnel expenses rose to EUR24.8 million, up 22.2% compared with 2024, in line with headcount growth. Average headcount increased by 28.6% to 216 employees (vs. 168 in 2024). As of 31 December 2025, the Group employed 231 people.

Depreciation and amortisation expenses increased to EUR4.7 million (vs. EUR2.4 million in 2024), reflecting B18 rig commissioning and a full year of depreciation of the B04 rig.

Net financial income amounted to EUR2.7 million (vs. EUR11.2 million in 2024), mainly due to lower income generated from cash investments. It should be noted that in 2024, other financial income included the fair value remeasurement of Geoven's bank debt.

Net income came in at --EUR23.6 million (vs. --EUR11.1 million in 2024), and Group share of net income at --EUR20.7 million (vs. --EUR10.0 million in 2024).

  • An optimised investment plan

In 2025, Arverne selectively optimised its investment plan, particularly with respect to operations related to the Lithium de France project. As a result, investments amounted to EUR38.6 million, compared with an initial plan of approximately EUR50 million.

Of this total, EUR28.8 million mainly corresponds to expenditures incurred by Lithium de France for the drilling of the first geothermal doublet, and EUR4.8 million to the acquisition of drilling equipment.

As of 31 December 2025, the Company has a solid financial structure, with equity of EUR158.8 million (vs. EUR169.9 million as of 31 December 2024), net debt of --EUR41.0 million (vs. --EUR85.6 million as of 31 December 2024), and --EUR70 million excluding lease liabilities (vs. --EUR114 million as of 31 December 2024).

2025 NON-FINANCIAL RESULTS Arverne has confirmed its status as a Mission-driven company and continues to roll out innovative initiatives aimed at strengthening its performance and impact across its three pillars: environment, employees and regional footprint.

  • Environment -- Objective 1: a comprehensive carbon footprint was carried out, covering Scopes 1, 2 and 3. Carbon intensity decreased by 6% in 2025 (vs. 2024), in line with the 2026 reduction trajectory. Avoided emissions were tripled.
  • Employees -- Objective 5: the Group conducted its first employee engagement survey, marking a structuring milestone in listening to and engaging with employees. 89% of employees report being satisfied, reflecting a high level of engagement and well-being.
  • Region -- Objective 9: Arverne stepped up on-the-ground initiatives -- meetings with local residents, site visits and information sessions -- to support geothermal and lithium projects at the heart of local communities.

The achievements for 2025, across the Group's 10 mission objectives, are detailed in the 2025 Mission Report.

In addition, 95 Group employees became shareholders through the Free Share Allocation plan launched at the time of the IPO in 2023.

CAPITAL MARKET DAY — 26 MARCH 2026

Arverne will unveil its strategic plan and ambitions to contribute to the development of a robust and high-performing geothermal industry, addressing today's key energy sovereignty challenges. The financial outlook, together with the investment and financing plans, will be detailed at this event.

From 8:30 a.m. to 12:30 p.m.: Capital Market Day dedicated to institutional investors

DIGITAL EVENT — REGISTRATION

About ARVERNE

ARVERNE, is the leading French supplier of geothermal solutions.

It specialises in harnessing natural ground resources to transform them into local energy sources for heating and cooling systems and to extract lithium. A mission-driven company listed on Euronext Paris, Arverne works for local authorities and industrial companies with a focus on energy sovereignty and short supply chains.

www.arverne.earth

data.title: Stardust Power 2025 10-K: $0 Revenue, $(2.13) EPS on $(15.7M) Net Loss
data.description:
Stardust Power reported zero revenue for the year and posted a net loss of $15.724 million, or $(2.13) per share, for the year ended December 31, 2025; the company has not generated operating revenue to date and recorded an operating loss of $16.083 million. These results reflect ongoing pre‑commercial activities and investments in site acquisition, engineering and development rather than product sales. Management continues to advance preconstruction and permitting while pursuing additional capital and supply arrangements to support future operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 — Company has not generated any revenue to date.
  • Gross Profit: $0 — No manufacturing or cost of goods sold incurred since inception.
  • Operating loss: $(16.083) million — reported in consolidated statements.
  • Net loss: $(15.724) million — Net loss for the year ended December 31, 2025.
  • Net loss per share: $(2.13) — Basic and diluted net loss per share for 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Commercial readiness & site acquisition: Purchased a site in Muskogee, Oklahoma and completed FEL‑3 engineering; Phase 1 is scoped at 25k tpa with an anticipated ~24‑month construction timeline.
  • Supply partnerships: Executed non‑binding supply letters covering approximately 13.5k tpa LCE (Prairie, Mandrake) and a 20k tpa offtake letter with Sumitomo to support future feedstock and product placement.
  • Technology & engineering: Secured an exclusive KMX VMD license and completed Primero FEL‑3 work to optimize plant design, costs and efficiencies.
  • Capital formation enabling operations: Completed multiple equity and convertible financings in 2025 that funded engineering, land acquisition and preconstruction activities.
  • Operational outlook & risks: Projected refinery design can accept blended lithium chloride feedstocks; ramping to commercial production is contingent on permits, feed supply stability and additional capital raises.

Original SEC Filing: Stardust Power Inc. [ SDST ] - 10-K - Mar. 25, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Finward Bancorp 2025 10-K: $91.8M Interest Income; $1.88 Diluted EPS
data.description:
Finward Bancorp reported total interest income of $91.8 million for the fiscal year, driven primarily by loan and securities yields, and delivered diluted earnings per share of $1.88; however, net income declined to $8.087 million largely due to significantly lower non-interest gains versus the prior year. Net interest income rose 17.1% to $56.7 million, reflecting improved net interest margin from higher loan yields and lower funding costs. The company continues to prioritize liquidity and credit monitoring amid a concentrated commercial real estate portfolio and ongoing regulatory oversight.

Financial Highlights

  • Total interest income: $91.8 million (primary revenue driver from loans and securities)
  • Net interest income: $56.7 million, up 17.1% year‑over‑year, reflecting an improved net interest margin
  • Total non‑interest income: $9.298 million, declined significantly versus 2024 due to a one‑time gain in 2024
  • Net income: $8.087 million, decreased 33.3% year‑over‑year largely because of lower non‑interest gains
  • Diluted earnings per share: $1.88 for 2025

Business Highlights

  • Net interest margin expansion: Improvement driven by higher loan yields and reduced deposit and borrowing costs, which strengthened core earnings.
  • Deposit mix shift: Customer balances moved toward more liquid accounts—money market balances increased while certificates of deposit declined—affecting funding composition and liquidity management.
  • Commercial real estate concentration: CRE remains the largest portfolio segment at 38% of loans; management has heightened monitoring due to occupancy and hybrid‑work risks.
  • Liquidity and funding capacity: Strong liquidity position with $673.9 million available through cash, securities and borrowing lines to support loan commitments and operations.
  • Regulatory and operational oversight: A previously issued consent order has been resolved, though an ongoing memorandum of understanding requires quarterly regulatory progress reporting and limits on dividend actions.

Original SEC Filing: Finward Bancorp [ FNWD ] - 10-K - Mar. 25, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Central Bancompany reports $1,021.4M revenue and $1.75 diluted EPS in 2025 10-K
data.description:
Central Bancompany reported total revenue of $1,021.4 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, driven by net interest income of $789.7 million and noninterest income of $231.7 million, and delivered GAAP net income of $390.9 million, equal to diluted earnings per common share of $1.75. The results reflect higher net interest income and fee growth alongside portfolio repositioning and efficiency gains. Central Bancompany noted improvements in operating efficiency and expanded wealth and brokerage activities supporting noninterest income diversification.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $1,021.4 million (Net interest income $789.7M + Noninterest income $231.7M)
  • Operating income: $506.6 million (income before income taxes)
  • Net income: $390.9 million (GAAP net income for year ended Dec 31, 2025)
  • Net income per common share - diluted: $1.75

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and portfolio strategy: Repositioned the investment portfolio toward higher-yield securities and reduced low-yield cash balances to support net interest income growth.
  • Distribution footprint: Operates 155 full-service branches across 11 primary markets, and completed an IPO with Nasdaq listing to broaden market presence.
  • Technology and efficiency: Invested in a new core banking platform and other technology initiatives, contributing to an improved efficiency ratio of 49.5% through cost controls.
  • Wealth and fee momentum: Wealth assets under administration grew 18%, boosting brokerage and fiduciary fees and diversifying noninterest income streams.

Original SEC Filing: Central Bancompany, Inc. [ CBC ] - 10-K - Mar. 25, 2026

data.title: Jefferies Says Private-Lending Blowups Led to $17 Million in Losses — WSJ
data.description:
By Ben Glickman

Jefferies Financial Group, a focus of investors fearing the fallout from prominent private-lending blowups, disclosed $17 million in losses from two high-profile lending collapses.

The losses were related to the collapse of auto-parts supplier First Brands and U.K. mortgage company Market Financial Solutions, both of which took on large debt from nonbank lenders. Jefferies said its exposure to First Brands was now zero, after disclosing a new $10 million pre-tax loss.

Investors appeared satisfied with that figure, as well as other financial results reported after the market closed Wednesday. Shares were up slightly in post-market trading.

The investment bank has drawn more attention in recent months as investors fears mount about the private-lending industry, owing in part to concerns about fraud and fears about exposure to software companies that could be disrupted by artificial intelligence. Many investors in private-debt funds have flocked to the exits.

"Management is disappointed and takes full responsibility for the losses already recognized and that may be absorbed over time in respect of First Brands, all of which are manageable," Chief Executive Richard Handler and President Brian Friedman said in written comments with first-quarter results.

Jefferies previously disclosed a $30 million pre-tax loss connected to First Brands. Jefferies's revenue and profit both rose in the first quarter from a year earlier. Executives said results were boosted by market-share gains, as well as strong merger-and-acquisition and equity-markets activity.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: US links security guarantees to Ukraine giving up Donbas, Zelenskiy says
data.description:
Key points:
  • US has offered security guarantees if Ukraine hands Donbas region to Russia, Zelenskiy says
  • Ukrainian leader says abandoning defensive line in Donbas would be a major security risk
  • Zelenskiy thanks Trump for continuing US weapon supplies to Ukraine despite Iran conflict
  • Ukraine's advances in missile, drone production allow it to retaliate against Russian air strikes

By Daniel Flynn and Olena Harmash

The U.S. is making its offer of security guarantees for a peace deal in Ukraine conditional on Kyiv ceding all of the country's eastern region of Donbas to Russia, President Volodymyr Zelenskiy told Reuters in an interview.

With the U.S. focused on its own conflict with Iran, President Donald Trump is applying pressure to Ukraine in an effort to bring a quick end to the four-year war triggered by Russia's 2022 invasion, Zelenskiy said.

"The Middle East definitely has an impact on President Trump, and I think on his next steps. President Trump, unfortunately, in my opinion, still chooses a strategy of putting more pressure on the Ukrainian side," he told Reuters.

Hundreds of thousands of people have been killed, and swaths of Ukraine laid waste, in Europe's bloodiest conflict since World War Two.

The Ukrainian leader has repeatedly said robust security guarantees from international partners are needed to ensure that Russia does not restart hostilities in the future, after any peace deal is agreed.

Two vital questions remained unresolved regarding security guarantees, Zelenskiy said: Who would help to fund Ukraine's weapons purchases to sustain its military deterrent, and how exactly would its allies respond in the face of any future Russian aggression?

"The Americans are prepared to finalise these guarantees at a high level once Ukraine is ready to withdraw from Donbas," the 48-year-old leader said.

Russian President Vladimir Putin insists that control of the whole of Donbas is an essential element of his war aims, which Moscow would achieve on the battlefield if it could not do so at the negotiating table.

But the pace of Russia's advance has been slow over the past two years. Military analysts say it could take a long time and a significant amount of manpower to conquer all of Donbas, which includes a so-called Fortress Belt of cities heavily fortified by the Ukrainian military.

Zelenskiy warned that a withdrawal would compromise the security of both Ukraine and, by extension, Europe, by handing the region's strong defensive positions to Russia.

"I would very much like the American side to understand that the eastern part of our country is part of our security guarantees," he said.

The White House did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Zelenskiy had said in January that a security guarantees document between Ukraine and the U.S. was "100% ready" and waiting to be signed. On Tuesday, following weekend talks between U.S. and Ukrainian officials in Miami, he said there was still work to be done.

RUSSIA IS BETTING U.S. WILL WALK AWAY, ZELENSKIY SAYS

Speaking in a gilded meeting room at the presidential offices in central Kyiv, Zelenskiy said Russia was betting Washington would lose interest if the peace talks stalled and would walk away. He acknowledged that there was some risk of this.

Zelenskiy questioned, however, whether Russia was willing to sacrifice hundreds of thousands more soldiers in an effort to capture the area of Donbas it does not already control - roughly 6,000 square km. He repeated that a summit with Trump, Putin and himself was the only way to settle outstanding questions on territory and security guarantees to clinch a peace deal.

The Ukrainian leader shrugged off past tensions between himself and Trump. "I am not a box of chocolates or a car, to be liked or disliked by one person or another," he said. "In my opinion, the President of the United States looks at this more pragmatically, and he probably wants the war to end quickly. We also want to do so quickly."

Following heavy Russian bombardment of Ukrainian cities on Wednesday, Zelenskiy thanked the Trump administration for maintaining the supplies of Patriot missile defence systems, despite increased demand for these weapons because of the conflict in the Gulf.

Ukrainian officials have previously expressed fears that shipments of U.S.-made Patriots - the only missiles in Ukraine's arsenal capable of downing Russian ballistic missiles - would dry up because of the Iran conflict.

"Deliveries to us were not stopped. I'm very grateful to President Trump, and to his team," Zelenskiy said. "But this supply of Patriot missiles is not as large as we need."

Meanwhile, Ukraine was making progress in its production of its own long-range missiles and drones, allowing it to strike deep within Russia in retaliation for Russian bombardment of Ukrainian cities, Zelenskiy said.

data.title: This Depressed Fund Owns a Stake in SpaceX. An IPO Could Give It a Big Lift. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Andrew Bary

One little-known holder of SpaceX stock is Blue Owl Technology Finance, a depressed business development company focused on high-yield technology lending in the private credit market.

The fund, managed by Blue Owl, a leading private-credit specialist, owned about 500,000 shares of SpaceX that were valued at around $195 million at year-end. The investment is disclosed in its filings including the 2025 10-K.

The current value could be much higher because Blue Owl was carrying the stake at a SpaceX valuation of about $720 billion, against a potential IPO valuation of $1.75 trillion. At that higher valuation, the Blue Owl stake could be worth close to $500 million.

There has been rising investor enthusiasm Wednesday for SpaceX after a report in the Information that the Elon Musk-controlled company could file a prospectus for its IPO as early as this week.

Investors may not be giving the Blue Owl fund credit for the SpaceX investment, which is admittedly small relative to the Blue Owl Technology Finance's investment portfolio of $14 billion. At a $1.75 billion valuation, the SpaceX investment would be worth about $1 a share given some 464 million shares outstanding.

Blue Owl Technology Finance shares (OTF) are up 2.4% to $12.70 Wednesday but are down 13% this year and trade for a roughly 27% discount to the BDC's net asset value o f more than $17 a share on Dec. 31. Private-credit BDCs issue shares to investors which then trade publicly at a premium or discount to NAV based on demand.

EchoStar, which owns a much more sizable stake in SpaceX that is valued at more than $25 billion, has seen its shares gain almost 9% Wednesday to $120.51. SpaceX accounts for much of the value in EchoStar.

Investors have shunned the Blue Owl BDC — and private-credit BDCs generally — this year because of concerns about private credit and technology loans in particular.

Blue Owl Technology Finance executives talked about the SpaceX investment on the fund's earnings conference call this quarter. The fund made a well-times investment of $27 million in SpaceX in 2021, leaving it with a huge gain. The fund mostly owns private-credit loans and also holds preferred and common shares investments as do many private-credit BDCs both public and private.

Erik Bissonnette, the company's president, said on the February call:

"So I believe it's marked at around $720 billion or so today. That obviously does not contemplate the merger of SpaceX and xAI, which was consummated in Q1 at a $1.25 trillion value. I keep stumbling over that word because it's such a staggeringly large number. So we would obviously expect to see some meaningful — expect to see a meaningful uptick in that position in Q1 as well."

Write to Andrew Bary at andrew.bary@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Gold Has Tarnished Lately. These Analysts See a Breakout Moment. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Janet H. Cho

Gold prices have fallen sharply in the month since the outbreak of the conflict in Iran, as have funds tracking the commodity and mining companies. But conditions are building for a breakout movement higher, according to SentimenTrader analysts.

The commodity gold is down 13.01% this month, and on pace for its worst month since October 2008, according to Dow Jones Market Data. Likewise the SPDR Gold Shares exchange-traded fund, or GLD, is down 14.6% month to date, and the VanEck Gold Miners ETF, or GDX, is down 25%.

Designed to track the price of bullion, the SPDR GLD fund just touched an extreme level, the analysts said in a note, adding that after similar sentiment washouts, gold has rebounded. "Gold bugs can hang onto the idea that quick bear markets haven't been a bad time to bet on a rebound, " they said.

They tracked sentiment around GLD through past gold downturns, specifically when at least 80% of traders were bearish for a two-week period, and found a pattern. About 89% of the time, the GLD fund's performance rebounded over the next 12 months when sentiment reached this low level, the analysts found, with median returns over 10%.

Similarly, the SentimenTrader analysts also tracked mining stocks as represented by the GDX. A month ago, very few of these stocks were in a bear market, defined as down at least 20% from a recent high. Now, 95% of the components are in a bear market.

"We believe this combination of severe price compression, completely exhausted market breadth, and extreme pessimism is not a cause for panic, but rather a classic contrarian trading opportunity," they wrote.

Gold recently saw a rapid decline of about 18.5% from its 52-week highs, and prices have been volatile because of escalating geopolitical events. But the flushout of gold presents what SentimenTrader calls "a very compelling quantitative setup."

Given that GLD's 200-day moving average remains intact, and historical data "strongly suggests that 'weak-handed investors' have been shaken out, this creates a highly asymmetric and high-probability entry point for a long position over the next 3 to 12 months."

Write to Janet H. Cho at janet.cho@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: AACG: Revenue flat for FY 2025, but net loss widened amid competition and goodwill impairment
data.description:
Q4 2025 saw an 11.7% revenue decline and a net loss due to lower portfolio training revenues and a one-time goodwill impairment. FY 2025 revenues were flat, but net loss widened as competition increased and margins fell. Cash position improved after an $8.85M ADS offering.

Original document: ATA Creativity Global [AACG] SEC 6-K Current Report — Mar. 26 2026

data.title: SWAG: Revenue up 40.6% to $116.2M; net loss narrows to $0.7M; strong growth and improved margins
data.description:
Revenue grew 40.6% to $116.2M in 2025, driven by organic growth and the Gander Group acquisition. Net loss narrowed to $0.7M, with improved operating margins and strong liquidity. Ongoing risks include tariffs, competition, and internal control remediation.

Original document: Stran & Company, Inc. [SWAG] SEC 10-K Annual Report — Mar. 26 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Village Super Market hit with Wakefern counterclaims seeking exit from cooperative
data.description:
Village Super Market said Wakefern filed counterclaims seeking a declaration to compel it to sell its Wakefern stock and exit the cooperative.

Key Highlights:

  • Wakefern filed counterclaims and a third-party complaint on March 19, 2026, seeking a declaration of good cause to force Village Super Market to sell its stock and exit the cooperative.
  • Village Super Market says Wakefern has not alleged a board vote to compel the sale and believes there is no basis in the By-Laws for such action.
  • The company calls Wakefern's claims frivolous, intends to seek dismissal, and says the dispute has not materially impacted operations or financial performance to date.
  • Village Super Market cannot currently estimate the probability of outcome or any range of potential loss related to the litigation.

Original SEC Filing: VILLAGE SUPER MARKET INC [ VLGEA ] - 8-K - Mar. 25, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Tevogen Bio Holdings regains Nasdaq bid-price compliance after 1-for-50 reverse split
data.description:
Tevogen Bio Holdings regained compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price rule following a 1-for-50 reverse stock split.

Key Highlights:

  • Nasdaq notified Tevogen Bio Holdings on March 20, 2026 that it has regained compliance with Rule 5450(a)(1).
  • The compliance restoration followed a 1-for-50 reverse split of the company's common stock effective March 6, 2026.
  • Nasdaq closed the matter following the Notification Letter, allowing continued listing on the Nasdaq Global Market.

Original SEC Filing: Tevogen Bio Holdings Inc. [ TVGN ] - 8-K - Mar. 25, 2026
